free_text: Absolute measurement of the 242Pu neutron-capture
cross section
The experiment was carried out at
LANSCE 800-MeV linear proton accelerator.
A double-sided, electroplated
target composed of 0.642 mg enriched 242Pu with an
active area 7.6 mm in diameter. 239Pu with an atomic
ratio of 5.0%, was added to the target to set the
absolute scale of the 242Pu neutron-capture cross
section using the well-known 239Pu(n,f) cross section
Gamma rays were measured using 4 Pi highly
segmented highly efficient gamma-ray calorimeter
DANCE. It consists of 160 BaF2 scintillation crystals,
and each one has the same solid-angle coverage. The
calorimeter has inner radius of 17 cm and each
crystal has length of 15 cm.
Parallel plate avalanche counter was used to
detect fission fragments and tag the gamma rays from
fission.
neutron energy determined by time of flight
method, measured by the time difference between the
beam pulse and the event detected by either DANCE or
PPAC. Two measurements were made using target with
total mass of 395 mu-g. In the second experiment target
with total mass of 374 mu-g was inserted into PPAC,
which was used in an attempt to measure neutron-induced
fission of 238Pu, which proved to be difficult.
Pulsed neutron beam was produced via
spallation on a natural W target by an 800-MeV proton
linear accelerator beam. Pulse repetition rate was
20 Hz. Neutrons were slowed down by a water moderator
Compiled by S.H.
Data presented in fig. 8 of the reference
sent by author (M.Q.B)
The (n,gamma) cross section was determined from the
spectrum of total gamma-ray energy versus multiplicity
measured by DANCE in the inclusive data collection mode
Statistical uncertainty
Data taken from tab. 1 of the reference
Resonances parameters for resonances with energies
between 2.66 and 495 eV were extracted to the first
order using the R-matrix code SAMMY
Statistical uncertainty
Systematic uncertainty in resonance energy
